mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.15% represents the zero lower bound constraint on monetary policy. When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero, the central bank has exhausted conventional rate-cutting tools and must rely on quantitative easing or forward guidance. This signals an emergency monetary regime that typically precedes equity market stabilization and recovery as unconventional stimulus measures take effect, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% on 2009-01-15 indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-2008.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window, forward equity returns decline as earnings expectations are revised downward and recession risk increases. The current UNRATE of 7.6% reflects ongoing labor market stress from the financial crisis.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% on 2009-01-15 reflects crisis-era unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising UNRATE, or correlation between UNRATE change and forward returns exceeds -0.10 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) measure consumer spending, the largest component of aggregate demand. A sustained decline in RSAFS signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced corporate earnings prospects. When RSAFS falls below its 6-month moving average, equity valuations compress as growth expectations are revised downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344,649M on 2009-01-15 reflects depressed consumer spending
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances when RSAFS is below its 6-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic output and manufacturing capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals contraction in the real economy and reduced corporate profitability. The current INDPRO of 103.60 (index 2017=100) reflects the sharp production collapse during the 2008-2009 recession. Continued INDPRO weakness predicts negative equity returns as earnings are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.60 on 2009-01-15 reflects recession-era production decline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when INDPRO declines > 1% MoM, or correlation between INDPRO change and forward returns is < 0.08.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly determine labor market slack. When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises simultaneously, the labor market is in acute distress, signaling severe earnings headwinds and recession deepening. This interaction effect produces stronger negative equity returns than either series alone because it confirms both job destruction and reduced hiring capacity.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 200K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134,580K and UNRATE at 7.6% on 2009-01-15 indicate severe labor market stress
regime_hint: acute labor market deterioration during financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of instances when both PAYEMS and UNRATE trigger conditions are met, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer and construction demand. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand destruction across housing and consumption, indicating severe recession conditions. This interaction produces stronger negative equity returns because it reflects synchronized weakness across multiple demand channels rather than isolated sector stress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 600K units annualized AND RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 550K and RSAFS at 344,649M on 2009-01-15 reflect housing and consumption collapse
regime_hint: synchronized demand collapse in housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 64% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS trigger conditions are met, or interaction term adds less than 5% explanatory power beyond additive effects.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly determine real monetary conditions. When FEDFUNDS is near zero while CPIAUCSL is stable or declining, real interest rates are highly negative, creating a powerful incentive for equity investment as cash returns are deeply negative. This regime-specific mechanism is strongest during deflationary or low-inflation crises when nominal rates cannot fall further.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% and CPIAUCSL at 211.49 on 2009-01-15 indicate zero rates with low inflation
regime_hint: zero lower bound with deflationary pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL trigger conditions are met, or real rate proxy fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand and economic output. In the post-crisis recovery phase, when GDPC1 growth turns positive after contraction, equity markets anticipate earnings recovery and produce positive forward returns. The current GDPC1 of 11,599.4B (Q4 2008) reflects the trough of the recession; subsequent quarters showing GDPC1 expansion signal recovery onset and justify positive equity positioning.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0% (positive growth after contraction)
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,599.4B on 2008-10-15 reflects recession trough
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when GDPC1 turns positive, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is < 0.12 over any 3-year rolling window post-2009.
